rows.definition: A write-time (post-tool) gate on tool RESULTS that decides if bytes enter context as-is, must be quarantined, or paged out to a pointer.
rows.distinction: Gates RESULT BYTES on the text side - NOT KV-MMU (which evicts K/V spans on the attention side) and NOT recall (the persisted core dump).

rows.definition: The bridge that turns ctxmmu's logical quarantine verdict into a mechanical one by evicting K/V spans from the kernel's attention cache.
rows.distinction: Evicts K/V spans on the ATTENTION side - NOT context-MMU (which gates result bytes on the text side) and NOT the KV cache (the storage it evicts from).

rows.definition: The persisted session core-dump: a page table over a content-addressed swap device, reloadable in a fresh process where a sealed page stays sealed.
rows.distinction: PERSISTENCE of a finished session - NOT compaction (provider prefix reuse on the wire) and NOT context-MMU (the live result gate).

rows.definition: Provider prefix reuse on the wire: re-sending a byte-identical prefix so the provider serves it from its prompt cache instead of re-prefilling.
rows.distinction: REUSE on the wire - NOT recall (persisting a finished session) and NOT context-MMU (gating result bytes into context).
gaps: compaction vs provider prompt-cache reuse is defined-only

rows.definition: The planner that forecasts the bounded resident working set of context spans for a session and learns from witnessed attention outcomes.
rows.distinction: PLANS the resident working set - NOT context-MMU (the per-result admission gate) and NOT recall (the persisted image).
gaps: the ctx* planner family is defined-only, not yet in the glossary

rows.definition: On-demand context materializer: turns a search query into typed handles, materialization verdicts, omissions, and a render plan over CDB images.
rows.distinction: The MATERIALIZER that turns a query into handles - NOT ctxplan (the optimizer that picks which spans keep resident under budget).

rows.definition: Stateless, shared context-view planner wired to the gateway: one per server, shared across all requests, disabled by default.
rows.distinction: The STATELESS shared planner seam - NOT SessionPlanner (the stateful per-session planner) and NOT ctxplan (the optimizer it drives).
gaps: stateless CtxViewPlanner vs stateful SessionPlanner is defined-only

rows.definition: A LOSSY compaction model: strips recovery handles off elided spans to show token savings without recoverability.
rows.distinction: The LOSSY savings model - NOT compaction (provider prefix reuse on the wire) and NOT recall (the lossless core dump).
gaps: CompactionView (lossy model) vs compaction (wire reuse) is defined-only

rows.definition: Transient state for brainstorm epochs: (Speculative, Epoch, ParentEpoch) marking provisional effects produced under speculation.
rows.distinction: Provisional brainstorm-epoch STATE - NOT the context-MMU trust decision and NOT a persisted recall page.
gaps: SpeculationContext (epoch state) vs the trust gate is defined-only

rows.definition: Negative-only context mutation request: one persisted recall-page tombstone with reason, requestor, and witness.
rows.distinction: A negative-only TOMBSTONE request - NOT contextq (which materializes spans IN) and NOT recall (the store it tombstones against).

rows.definition: Self-describing proof of a recall cost calculation: shows the gate's work (PrefixTokens, UnitTokens, ReadMult, siblings, cost breakdown).
rows.distinction: The PROOF artifact of a recall cost gate - NOT ProveRecall (the function that produces it) and NOT recall (the subsystem it prices).

rows.definition: Pure function that runs the recall cost gate over its inputs and returns a RecallProof showing the gate's work.
rows.distinction: The pure FUNCTION that computes the proof - NOT RecallProof (the artifact it returns) and NOT recall (the subsystem).

rows.definition: A planned, budgeted view of context the CtxViewPlanner selects for one turn within a token budget.
rows.distinction: The SELECTED VIEW for a turn - NOT CtxViewPlanner (which selects it) and NOT contextq (which materializes raw handles).
gaps: CtxView (the view) vs CtxViewPlanner (the selector) is defined-only

rows.definition: The model's hard token budget that the planner allocates resident spans within.
rows.distinction: The hard token BUDGET - NOT ctxplan (which allocates within it) and NOT recall (the overflow store it spills to).
gaps: context window (budget) vs ctxplan (allocator) is defined-only

rows.definition: The applied negative mutation: tombstones a recall page through the trust gate per a ContextChangeRequest.
rows.distinction: The APPLIED tombstone effect - NOT ContextChangeRequest (the request record it consumes) and NOT recall (the page store it mutates).
gaps: ContextChange (apply) vs ContextChangeRequest (the request) is defined-only
